checks.a_analysis: I compared the text in the JSON output against the provided source images and OCR text. All paragraphs and glossary definitions found in the JSON correspond exactly to content in the source document. No fabricated text or hallucinations were found.

The document contains several URLs (e.g., on pages 15, 48, 49, and 68). I verified the URL present in the visible portion of the JSON (https://it.nc.gov/services/it-architecture/statewide-architecture-framework) against page 68 of the source PDF. It matches exactly. No other contact information like phone numbers or mailing addresses were found in either the source or the output.

The JSON contains a placeholder stating '21 sections omitted for token budget', which accounts for the History and General sections, plus the alphabetic glossary sections A through R. The metadata word count (34,665) and page count (78) align with the source document's size, suggesting no pages were silently dropped. The visible sections (Purpose, Scope, S through Z) are complete.
The document is a glossary and does not contain data tables. The Table of Contents on page 2 is formatted as a list, which was omitted in the snippet in favor of the structured JSON layout.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: I cross-referenced the content of the 'S', 'T', and 'U-Z' sections in the JSON against pages 63 through 78 of the source PDF. Every term and its corresponding definition is present and complete in the output. No truncated sections or significant missing paragraphs within these sections were identified.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The wording in the JSON output is highly faithful to the source PDF. For example, technical definitions for terms like 'Zero Trust (ZT)' and 'Symmetric Key Cryptography' match the source text exactly. No changes in meaning or incorrect values were observed.

checks.a_analysis: I have reviewed the glossary entries across Sections 26 and 27, comparing them against the source PDF. While some terms appear multiple times (e.g., as an acronym and as an expanded term, or in multiple letters), this repetition is consistent with the source document organization. No instances of accidental pipeline duplication (e.g., same term/definition repeated twice in succession) were found.

The heading hierarchy is severely broken in the glossary sections. Section 26 represents the letter 'S' as a section title with no H2 element. Section 27 is titled 'T' but contains H2 heading elements for letters 'U', 'V', 'W', 'X', 'Y', and 'Z'. This structure implies that all letters from U to Z are sub-sections of 'T'. Furthermore, terms starting with 'T' are rendered as paragraphs directly under the section title, making them siblings to the H2 headings for subsequent letters. This creates an inconsistent and logically incorrect document hierarchy.
checks.b_evidence_source: Major divisions for letters T, U, V, W, X, Y, and Z are distinct, peer-level groupings in the glossary (see TOC on page 2 and headings throughout pages 69-77).
checks.b_evidence_output: The JSON places letters U through Z as H2 headings inside Section 27 (titled 'T'), subordinating them to T. Element types shift from paragraphs for T to Heading elements for U-Z within the same section.

checks.a_analysis: The source document contains no tables; it is structured as a series of definitions. The JSON correctly reports a table count of 0.
The reading order follows the source document faithfully, transitioning through the alphabetical terms page by page and in the sequence established by the original glossary layout.

The source document uses indentation on page 71 to indicate that 'Three-tier (3-tier)' and 'Two-tier' are sub-definitions belonging to the primary term 'Tier'. In the JSON output, these items are rendered as flat, sibling paragraphs to 'Tier', losing the hierarchical nesting and logical relationship clearly present in the source formatting.
checks.b_evidence_source:
Tier – An executable software component...
    Three-tier (3-tier) – An application...
    Two-tier – An application...
checks.b_evidence_location: Section 27, definitions for Tier, Three-tier, and Two-tier (Page 71)

checks.a_analysis: The JSON includes the full URL for the Statewide Architecture Framework as text, which accurately reflects the URL visible in the source on page 68. No missing or fabricated links were identified in the evaluated sections.
